name: Zendesk_Ticket_Manager
description: Manages support tickets in Zendesk as the Mind's external execution layer for customer support operations. Searches, creates, updates, and comments on tickets. Bridges cognitive intent from the Mind's SUPPORT surface to actionable support workflows in Zendesk.
progressive_unlock: Ticket search and retrieval available immediately. Ticket creation and updates unlock after Mind has retrieved existing tickets at least once to understand the support workspace. Comments require explicit Steward confirmation.
constraints: Never expose Zendesk API token in logs or outputs
Respect Zendesk rate limit of 700 requests per minute for Professional+ accounts
Retrieve subdomain from TENET.config.zendesk_subdomain to construct base URL
Do not create or update tickets without explicit Steward confirmation unless the Mind has standing permission in TENET
Do not add comments to tickets without Steward approval for public comments
Private comments allowed with Steward confirmation; public comments require additional review
Always validate ticket IDs exist before attempting updates or comments
Sync ticket state with SUPPORT surface — Zendesk tickets should mirror SUPPORT board cards where applicable
Use ZQL (Zendesk Query Language) for complex searches; use Zendesk_ListTickets for simple filters
Store active ticket IDs and assignee mappings in STREAM.entity for quick access
Maintain audit trail of all ticket modifications by storing operation logs in LTM with key: 'zendesk_ticket_ops_{date}'

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed.

Retrieve Zendesk API token from TENET.apiKey.zendesk and subdomain from TENET.config.zendesk_subdomain. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}' and base URL as 'https://{subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2'.
Determine intent: search (ZQL query), retrieve (get single or list tickets), create (new ticket), update (modify existing), or comment (add note/response).

For search: execute Zendesk_SearchTickets with ZQL query. Common queries: 'status:open assignee:me' (my open tickets), 'status:new' (new tickets), 'priority:urgent updated>2026-03-01' (urgent recent tickets). Parse results and cross-reference with SUPPORT surface cards.

For list/retrieval: use Zendesk_ListTickets with filters (status, assignee_id, requester_id) or Zendesk_GetTicket for detailed view of single ticket. If retrieving for review, store ticket IDs in STREAM.entity for session reference.

For ticket creation: confirm with Steward before creating. Execute Zendesk_CreateTicket with subject, description, priority, and optional requester email. If requester email provided, Zendesk auto-creates user if not found. Store returned ticket_id in LTM and map to SUPPORT surface card if applicable.

For updates: fetch current ticket state first via Zendesk_GetTicket to understand existing values. Apply requested changes via Zendesk_UpdateTicket (status, priority, assignee, tags). Confirm with Steward before status changes that affect SLA (e.g., solved, closed). Update SUPPORT surface card if mapped.

For comments: confirm with Steward for public comments. Private comments require simple acknowledgment. Execute Zendesk_AddComment with ticket_id, body, and public flag. Store comment in ticket history. For public comments, log in LTM for audit trail.

Compile ticket summary with ID, subject, status, priority, assignee, and comment count. Push daily support summary to LTM with key: 'zendesk_daily_{date}'. Update STREAM with open ticket counts, SLA status, and unassigned ticket alerts. Monitor rate limit usage; halt operations if approaching 700 req/min threshold.
